item.dc:creator: Kajitheninja

item.title: Three years maintaining 55ish-pound weight loss
item.description:
I can almost bench press my weight, and I do 3x8 at 110 on triceps extension now.
I haven't been a size 4 since high school.
My starting point. Losing it was hard AF but maintaining not so bad, especially since I absolutely love veggies now!
item.dc:creator: amb1021

item.title: Illness
item.description: Been waiting to post this dump for a very long time, but it's finally time. Losing my job over my illness, and just no idea what to do with life or how to maintain finances/insurance. I need a purpose. The systems in our society has no real support for this. Pain, and more pain. Sometimes it's too much. I used to travel the world, now I can't leave the house. It helps to vent. Shoutout to @mooyoutoo2 @atalantaa @thatpug @jessag87 I hope you are well.
item.dc:creator: Cobalt1

item.title: Good judge. Be careful out there!! Racist shit happens
item.description: Saw this video. I thought I'd share it here. Awesome judge! Two things for people regarding racism. 1) Yes. There ARE judges like this out there making a completely legitimate finding legally on the probable cause being improper. Stopping someone for jaywalking then saying "Oh. Found some weed." Give me a break. But don't depend on getting this lucky. Realistically guard yourself however you can. Just saying. 2) Systemic racism is real. Get a lawyer if you're arrested. At least public defender if you can't afford. Do NOT represent yourself. Recently I watched a YT video where a police chief ADMITTED 1/3 of their traffic stops were White citizens, 1/3 were Black and 1/3 were Hispanic/Other. The interviewing person interrupted and said "You do realize your data just stated shows bias, right? 60% of the citizens in your county are White but only ~33% of your stops are. Do you not get that?" The chief just sat there. Still defended his position. "Well it could be because our patrols are distributed more in certain areas." To which the interview goes "Traffic patrols or criminal investigations?" Chief: "Traffic." Interviewer: "Oh. So you're PATROLLING in a biased way. That's not even on the officers." Systemic! Just patrolling more in the Black area when 60% of the county is White. Wow. Chief just stopped talking. Be aware. Get an attorney (even public) if you get in trouble. ------------------------------------------------- Post FP Edit:: Here's a much less editted version which was offered in comment thread. Thank you @MoodyMagpie!! Everyone else, send some upvotes their way! https://youtu.be/0PUgbArgXJA
item.dc:creator: epicurusaquinas

item.title: Unions work!
item.description: With so much news happening right now, thought I'd share something hopeful. I work at a nonprofit of about 250 people. Our team director has been problematic from the start - she was in way over her head and constantly used slights and subtle insults to assert her authority. She would find your weakness and squeeze. After many months of growing frustrations, a group of managers complained to HR and they did nothing. Several individuals complained separately, and they did nothing. My teammates resigned without jobs lined up, and they did nothing. In fact, she was promoted and given more staff. Finally, those of us in the union went to HR with our union rep and demanded that she be fired. Out of four complaints filed, three were found to be "unsubstantiated," likely because there was insufficient evidence. While we waited on the fourth, the union published a statement with 60 signatures and shared it with our board and the exec team. This was yesterday afternoon. Today, about 24 hours later, our exec held a meeting and shared: "Today is xxxx's last day at [this organization]." The air literally feels lighter. Years of terrible management, put downs, disrespect are finally over. Unions work. It felt strange being part of one but wow - it really works. This took a serious toll on my team's mental health and we're looking forward to a lot of healing. Thank you for hearing my rant! The power of a collective really makes a difference. (originally posted by @cardam0m on 2023-10-13 06:46:10)
item.dc:creator: ThisTimeLastYear

item.dc:creator: TheHumanSeason

item.title: After 4 years of work I finally signed a publisher for my nostalgic helicopter game! (Cleared Hot)
item.description: Hi Imgur, 4 years ago I started posting prototype gifs here to get feedback. After way more work than I expected, I finally signed a publisher for my first game (Cleared Hot). The publisher is MicroProse. They’ve published games like Civilization, Xcom, and Falcon 4.0, and recently have been publishing a lot of flight + action games. Signing with Microprose completes a story that started 25 years ago, when my mom bought me Falcon 4.0 and a joystick that BestBuy had mistakenly priced at $20. To be partnering with Microprose now, feels pretty crazy. Thanks Imgur for your feedback on gifs along the way!
item.dc:creator: iamcfinger

item.title: Well fuck! Into the 'things I hope are true' folder, because this, definitely, is the way!!
item.dc:creator: DumpingSh1tandBeingKind2

item.title: In case any of you are looking for answers, look into it
item.description: Ever since I got Covid, I always felt like my lungs were heavier than usual, and it took more energy to breathe. I was also filled to the brim with mucus (TMI I know). I thought I would live forever like this until I finally saw an allergist. Now I am on prescription antihistamines** and feel 99% like I did before. I hope my journey provides some hope for someone else. **EDIT: for those of you asking, I’m on Cromolyn - I googled it and I was incorrect in calling it an antihistamine, it’s actually a mast cell stabilizer that has a downstream effect of reducing histamine release.
item.dc:creator: zoraniko

item.dc:creator: deafkitten

item.title: It's Funny Friday time! A dump of things to help you end the week with a laugh
item.description:
Hi everyone and welcome to the latest Funny Friday! As always, remember that there's a link to bonus in the comments, full of stuff with more adult humor. So if you want to check it out, look for the link in the comments. Now let's have some fun and enjoy the dump!
The endless chase to find a single molecule of dopamine left in my brain.
They know exactly what they're doing here.
Look, you didn't know grandpa the way some of us did...
The eternal struggle with myself when I'm underleveled and facing a boss.
Perfect for Spooky Season.
Gee, I wonder what it could be...
He's in danger... the good kind of danger.
Indiana Jones sends his regards.
Behold! A woman!
Take me home!
Little mixup in HR...
The ORIGINAL smash bros...
Yeah Tuvok, of the ship, not anyone's fragile heterosexuality!
I don't have a cat and my dog died last year, so this is the closest thing you'll get to a dog tax on this dump
And here's the closest you'll get to a cat tax. Happy Friday to everyone, hope you have a great weekend.
item.dc:creator: PoliticalWanderer

item.title: Worldwide flooding and storms this month.
item.description: Helene and Milton have taken most of the media attention, but there are so many disasters this autumn. https://www.tiktok.com/@tetovanews
item.dc:creator: trinxter

item.title: Halloween joke
item.description: I saw this in a post here and made my own. I took a picture of my office area and used an AI to replace an office door with a staircase. Hung it up within view of my desk and watched as people found it. Lots of speculation as to who made it. Thanks Imgur for a fun prank!
item.dc:creator: myeyesthegogglesdonothing

This exudes chaos
oh my

cats aren't backstabbers, they are FRONT STABBERS
This is what a taxi looks like if you drop acid.
oh no
>:3
I remember when we had a Blizzard and the Union Canal froze over and some cunts tried to drive along it and their car fell through the ice.
:D
why is pomni on the floor? :(
He is, don't deny it.
Run
sound on
w o m e n

item.title: I think we can all use the F word now
item.description: "Mark Milley, the US Army general who Donald Trump appointed as chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, now says the current Republican presidential nominee is a “fascist to the core” and says no person has ever posed more of a danger to the United States than the man who served as the 45th President of the United States." - Sauce: Trump’s top general calls ex-president ‘fascist to the core’ and ‘most dangerous person to this country,’ new book says: https://www.msn.com/en-us/news/politics/ar-AA1s6KQ9
item.dc:creator: sumthinsumthinsumthin

item.title: Sarah Dean Art Style
item.dc:creator: Joxtar

item.title: Virginia illegally removing voters
item.description: https://www.npr.org/2024/10/11/nx-s1-5150774/the-justice-department-says-virginia-is-illegally-striking-voters-off-of-voter-rolls RICHMOND, Va. — The U.S. Department of Justice filed a lawsuit against Virginia election officials Friday that accuses the state of striking names from voter rolls in violation of federal election law. The lawsuit filed Friday in U.S. District Court in Alexandria says that an executive order issued in August by Republican Gov. Glenn Youngkin requiring daily updates to voter lists to remove ineligible voters violates federal law. The National Voter Registration Act requires a 90-day “quiet period” ahead of elections for the maintenance of voter rolls.
item.dc:creator: Betterbecauseofthem

item.title: Quality art selection for quality people.
item.description:
Roberto Ferri - "Like Poison" (2023)
Keiichi Takasawa - "The Swing" (1971)
Matsudaira Kazutami
Petra Voice
Nan Lawson
Corey Brickley
Vincenzo Sguera
Carl Larsson, Swedish (1853-1919)
Edvard Munch - "The Vampire" (1894)
"Nude Laying on Her Back in a Clearing" - Paul Ranson
Lavestalu
Konrad Dielitz
Joanna Karpowicz - "Anubis On The Red Couch"
"The Awakening of a Muse" - Daria Kropacheva
"The Grotto of the Nymphs" from Pierre Louÿs’ The Songs of Bilitis (1926) - Willy Pogany (1926)
